rulings.Case Title: STEVEN CHARLES PRICE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 29, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT DANA COMPANIES, L.L.C.'s, ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS DANA CORPORATION, MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 29, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT CRAWFORD AND COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JULY 15, 2008 PER PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: GREG MORRIS VS. SAFECO INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 29, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA, SUED SAFECO OF AMERICA, AS DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JULY 15, 2008 PER PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARVIN GEE VS. EUGENIO VASQUEZ MALLARI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Denying Application For Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 29, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANT LUXOR CAB COMPANY, INC.'S, Motion For Order Denying Application For Good Faith Settlement IS DENIED. IT APPEARS THAT FORNESI WAS PRIMARILY RESPONSIBLE FOR THE ACCIDENT AND THE SETTLEMENT DOES NOT BEAR A REASONABLE RELATIONSHIP TO THE ALLEGED DAMAGES. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: REGINA BIRKNER et al VS. KWAI HO LAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 29, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANT KWAI LAM'S Motion For Attorney'S Fees IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO RECOVER $62,087.50 AS REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE COURT HAS CONSIDERED PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS AND HAS REVIEWED THE PARTIES' DECLARATIONS. THE FACT THAT DEFENDANT HAS NOT SOUGHT A MULTIPLIER NOR COSTS FOR OTHER PERSONNEL OUTWEIGHS PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS FOR ANY EXCESS COSTS IN CONNECTION WITH THE DEPOSITIONS AND POST APPEAL HOURS. PLAINTIFF'S OTHER OBJECTIONS ARE NOT PERSUASIVE. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ROGER WOMACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS B, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 29, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT AUTOMATIC TRANSMISSION PARTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BY RELYING UPON AFFIRMATIVE EVIDENCE. PLAINTIIF HAS NOT PRODUCED EVIDENCE GIVING RISE TO A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE IS DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW THE EXISTENCE OF ANY EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM AND PLAINTIFF'S HAS FAILED TO EXPLAIN WHY THE REQUESTED DISCOVERY WAS NOT PREVIOUSLY OBTAINED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: AL SHAWA VS. JACK TAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Court Order Sustaining Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 29, 2008 LINE 6. CROSS-COMPLAINANT AL SHAWA'S MOTION To Vacate Court Order Sustaining Demurrer IS GRANTED. GIVEN PROOF OF REMOVAL TO FEDERAL COURT, THIS COURT LACKED JURISDICTION TO RULE ON THE DEMURRER. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. CHARLES KITHCART et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Charles Kithcart'S Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 29, 2008 LINE 19. DEFENDANT CHARLES KITHCART'S MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED. THE LAW FAVORS A TRIAL ON THE MERITS; PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED REASONS FOR THE DELAY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN T. DOHERTY VS. MICHAEL MENDELSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Determine Deft'S Status As Prevailing Party And To Fix Amount Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 29, 2008 LINE 16. DEFENDANT MICHAEL MENDELSON'S Motion To Determine DEFENDANT'S Status As Prevailing Party And To Fix Amount Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs IS DENIED BASED ON CIVIL CODE SECTION 1717 (B)(2) AND MITCHELLAND & IMPROVEMENT CO. V RISTORANTI FERRANTELLI, INC. (2007) 158 CAL APP 4TH 479. C.C.P. SECTION 128.7 IS THE APPLICABLE LAW NOT C.C.P. SECTION 128.6 AND THERE HAS BEEN NO SHOWING OF BAD FAITH. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: KWAI HO LAM VS. REGINA BIRKNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Tax Costs, Requests For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 29, 2008 LINE 17. PLAINTIFF KWAI LAM'S MOTION To Tax Costs (Request For Judicial NOTICE) IS DENIED. THE FACT THAT THE PARTIES HAD A STIPULATION AS TO THE USE OF THE DEPOSITION DOES NOT NEGATE DEFENDANTS' RIGHT TO RECOVER THE COSTS; EXPEDITING THE TRANSCRIPTS WAS REASONABLE AS WERE INTERPRETER COSTS. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CHANCE BEAUCHESNE VS. FRANK LIPKINS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Chance Beauchesne'S Motion To Vacate Stay
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MAY 29, 2008 LINE 20. PLAINTIFF CHANCE BEAUCHESNE'S MOTION TO VACATE STAY IS DENIED. THE COURT HAD JURISDICTION TO CONSIDER THE STAY UNDER C.C.P 1176.(A). ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT APPARENTLY DID NOT COMPLY WITH ALL OF THE LOCAL RULES IN SEEKING THE STAY, THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DESCRETION TO LEAVE THE STAY IN PLACE. =(302/PJM/PB)
